content:
My doctoral thesis is on representation learning for multi-relational data, and my thesis advisor is Pierre Vandergheynst. I like to research machine learning for graph-structured data.
I also have a signal processing background, which I majored in MSc. My master thesis was on the analysis of airborne LiDAR point clouds using the graph signal processing framework. I completed it under the co-supervision of Aydin Alatan and Elif Vural.
summary:
My doctoral thesis is on representation learning for multi-relational data, and my thesis advisor is Pierre Vandergheynst. I like to research machine learning for graph-structured data.
I also have a signal processing background, which I majored in MSc.
title: Eda Bayram

summary: I am looking for collaborators along this direction! Please [contact me](mailto:eda.bayram@epfl.ch) if you are interested. Also interested students (master or above) can contribute in the project defined [here](https://lts2.epfl.ch/projects/105).
title: Project: Heterogeneous MPNN on Knowledge graphs

summary: I have compiled some of the publicly available resources [here](https://www.notion.so/Graph-Representation-Learning-Resources-8f14d4bcf4a34af9b649efdf5e75eec0) and I try to keep it updated. Hope the students and graph enthusiasts can benefit!
title: Resources on Graph Machine Learning

summary: In this study, we address the incompleteness in the numerical node attributes of a knowledge graph. We propose the algorithm MrAP, Multi-Relational Attribute Propagation, which we formulate within a message passing scheme.
title: Node Attribute Completion in Knowledge Graphs with Multi-Relational Propagation

summary: The structure of the data very often depends on complex relationships of multiple types. In this study, our main research question is "Can we support the structure inference process with a priori relational information about the data domain?"
title: Mask Combination of Multi-layer Graphs for Global Structure Inference
